profile.personality: curious but reserved; thinks before replying; warms up after a few turns

transcript.content: Hi Priyansh, I'm Miss Maya. Honestly, I'm looking forward to this cricket match today, India vs Australia. What are you rooting for?
India, obviously! But Australia has some strong players too. The match should be close. Have you watched much cricket lately?
India, definitely! Close matches are the best, they keep me on edge. I get that you're a cricket fan. What position do you enjoy watching most?
Fast bowling is my favorite. The energy is so intense. I also like watching Virat Kohli bat — his focus is something else. Do you follow the T20s?
That sounds exciting. Fast bowling is full of energy. Virat Kohli is a great player. Which T20 team are you into?

I follow the Mumbai Indians a lot. They have a good mix of experienced and young players. But I also enjoy watching the Chennai Super Kings — they're like underdogs but always fight hard. Have you watched any T20 finals recently?
Mumbai Indians and Chennai Super Kings both have great energy. Underdogs who fight hard are always inspiring. Which T20 final stood out to you most?
The 2018 final between Chennai and Mumbai was epic. Rain, tie, then that super over — still gives me chills. I think "phoda" would be the word for it! Have you seen that match?
That sounds amazing, rain, a tie, and a super over! "Phoda" fits perfectly. What part of the match gave you the most chills?
The final moments of the super over — Jadhav running between the wickets, that catch by Krunal, it was *dhamaal*. I still remember the tension. Do you follow T20s or prefer Test cricket?
The final moments must have been wild, *dhamaal* is the right word! T20s or Tests? I'm curious what you like better.
Honestly, I like T20s more for the excitement and fast pace. Tests are long, and I have a busy work schedule. Still, I respect the skill it takes. How about you?
I get that, T20s are perfect for quick, exciting hits. Tests need a lot of time, which is hard to find. What’s your take on white ball cricket vs. Tests?
White ball cricket is more my style — shorter, more action. But Tests have their own respect. I can't spend hours on one match, though. How do you usually follow games? On TV, or do you watch live?
I get that, white ball cricket fits a busy schedule better. Watching on TV is easier for me. Do you usually watch full matches or just highlights?
I try to watch full matches if I can, but highlights are fine if I'm short on time. The T20s are easier to fit in, especially during the workweek. What about you? Do you prefer live games over TV?
I go with TV mostly, easier to pause and enjoy. Live games are fun if I can make time. Do you follow any specific players or just enjoy the team play?

I follow a few players — like Jasprit Bumrah for his bowling, and Suryakumar Yadav for his innovative batting. But I also enjoy how team strategies come together. Do you have a favorite player or team?
I like how you enjoy both individual skills and team play. Bumrah and Suryakumar are top talents. Do you have a favorite player you watch for their style?

Jasprit Bumrah’s yorkers and Suryakumar’s all-around batting really stand out. I also like how Shubman Gill plays — so calm and technical. But to be honest, I watch more for the team dynamics too. What about you, any player you follow closely?
Bumrah’s yorkers and Suryakumar’s batting are a treat to watch. Shubman Gill adds balance to the team. Do you follow any players for their unique style?

I follow players who bring something special — like Ravindra Jadeja’s all-round game. But honestly, the team chemistry is what keeps me hooked. I think I’ve said enough about cricket! How do you usually relax after a long day?
Team chemistry makes matches exciting, it’s a game within the game. Fair enough, you said enough! What do you do to relax after work?

moments.text: I love when a match ends with a tight finish, like a last-ball win. It gives me adrenaline.
I remember one match where India chased 300 in the last over with friends, we were all shouting. It's moments like that.
The 2004 semi-final between India and Sri Lanka (event), Sri Lanka won with last ball. Dil Chahta Hai (movie) is another special one. Both are special because they're underdog stories.

A friend recommended Dil Chahta Hai (movie). He said it's different. I watched it and really connected with the characters. It was not like other movies I was used to. From that day, it became my favorite.
